source_pr.title: planner, statistics, sessionctx: add variable to enable/disable the outdated statistics to pseudo logic
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #27601 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

The optimizer switches to pseudo statistics when the statistics on table is outdated, causing plan change and performance degradation.

```
mysql> explain select * from t where a between 1 and 10;
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
| id                     | estRows | task      | access object | operator info                                |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
| TableReader_6          | 0.15    | root      |               | data:TableRangeScan_5                        |
| └─TableRangeScan_5     | 0.15    | cop[tikv] | table:t       | range:[1,10], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------
2 rows in set (0.05 sec)
```

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
Introduce a session and global variable `tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats` to control if the optimizer uses pseudo for the outdated stats. By default the value is ON, pseudo is used for outdated stats, this behavior is consistent with previous release.
If `tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats` is set to OFF, when the table statistics is outdated, optimizer will stick to outdated stats, rather than using pseudo stats.

How it Works:
add a variable `tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats` to enable/disable the outdated statistics to pseudo logic

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code
```
use test;
create table t (a bigint(64), b bigint(64), primary key(a), index idx(b));
insert into t values (1, 3);
analyze table t;
insert into t values (2, 3);
insert into t values (5, 3);
insert into t values (8, 3);
insert into t values (12, 3);
// The statistic table is outdated now.
explain select * from t where a between 1 and 10;
mysql> show variables where variable_name like '%outdated%';
+---------------------------------------+-------+
| Variable_name                         | Value |
+---------------------------------------+-------+
| tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats | ON    |
+---------------------------------------+-------+

mysql> explain select * from t where a between 1 and 10;
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
| id                     | estRows | task      | access object | operator info                                |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
| TableReader_6          | 0.15    | root      |               | data:TableRangeScan_5                        |
| └─TableRangeScan_5     | 0.15    | cop[tikv] | table:t       | range:[1,10], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
2 rows in set (0.04 sec)

mysql>  set @@tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats=1;
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.04 sec)

mysql> explain select * from t where a between 1 and 10;
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
| id                     | estRows | task      | access object | operator info                                |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
| TableReader_6          | 0.15    | root      |               | data:TableRangeScan_5                        |
| └─TableRangeScan_5     | 0.15    | cop[tikv] | table:t       | range:[1,10], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+----------------------------------------------+
2 rows in set (0.03 sec)

mysql> set @@tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats=0;
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.04 sec)

mysql> explain select * from t where a between 1 and 10;
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+--------------------------------+
| id                     | estRows | task      | access object | operator info                  |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+--------------------------------+
| TableReader_6          | 6.00    | root      |               | data:TableRangeScan_5          |
| └─TableRangeScan_5     | 6.00    | cop[tikv] | table:t       | range:[1,10], keep order:false |
+------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+--------------------------------+
2 rows in set (0.04 sec)
```

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
Previously, the optimizer switches to pseudo statistics when the statistics on table is outdated,
in real-world workload, this behavior will probably cause plan change and performance degradation. 
With this pr, a session and global variable tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats is added, 
to control if the optimizer uses pseudo for the outdated stats. 
By default the value is 1, pseudo is used for outdated stats, this behavior is consistent with previous releases.
If tidb_enable_pseudo_for_outdated_stats is set to 0, when the table statistics is outdated, 
optimizer will stick to outdated stats, rather than using pseudo stats
```

evidence: evidence: // EnableStableResultMode if stabilize query results.
